zotero.abstractNote: Deep generative models are often criticized for being black boxes. While they can generate high-quality samples, it is often unclear how their internal representations relate to the causal structure of the data generating process. In this paper, we propose a framework to uncover the modular structure of deep generative models by leveraging counterfactual reasoning. We show that by intervening on the latent variables of a generative model, we can identify independent causal mechanisms that are learned by the model. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that it can recover the underlying causal structure of the data and provide interpretable explanations for the model's predictions.
